title: salix cinerea var. oleifolia
intro: Salix cinerea var. oleifolia, also known as the weeping willow or oleifera, is a variety of the grey willow (Salix cinerea) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Salix cinerea var. oleifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as having antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
